title: Campium lonchophorum
uses: Campium lonchophorum is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves and stems have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating fever and other related conditions. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and skin infections. Furthermore, Campium lonchophorum is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Campium lonchophorum.
benefits: I couldn't find any reliable sources on the health benefits of Campium lonchophorum.
properties: Unfortunately, I do not have information about the therapeutic properties of Campium lonchophorum.
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant 'Campium lonchophorum'. However, I found information on the plant 'Campylophyllum major' which is the same genus as Campium and is known as Campium lonchophorum in some references, but it seems to be an unresolved or misclassified name.

If the information I found is correct, Campylophyllum major is a type of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the moss family.

The liverwort Campylophyllum major has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. Its health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and enhance liver function.

The bioactive constituents of Campylophyllum major include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Campylophyllum major include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, liver and gallbladder disorders, and skin conditions.
